title: boundary_refined
description: Refined consensus breakpoint from the dosage-based breakpoint pipeline. Parallel to boundary_{left|right} (from STEP_C01g) but produced by 03_consensus_merge via weighted median over multiple per-method sources (ancestral_fragments, block_extension, c01j_transitions, step40_coherence, step41_switches, c01l_segments, step37_sv). CI is derived from the weighted MAD of agreeing methods. Written twice per candidate (boundary_refined_left, boundary_refined_right). Does NOT overwrite boundary_{side}; the two blocks coexist so downstream consumers can compare the refined estimate against the original C01g catalog. Long-format per-method table lives at breakpoints_per_method.json alongside.
question: Q3

final_bp.description: Consensus breakpoint position (weighted median over agreeing methods, in bp).

n_methods_total.description: Number of methods that contributed an estimate (available sources).

n_methods_agreeing.description: Count of methods within 2 × weighted-MAD of final_bp.

primary_source.description: Highest-weight method that was available and within the agreement band.

sources_available.description: List of method names that supplied an estimate for this side.

refined_vs_input_shift_kb.description: Signed shift from the originally-registered candidate boundary (start_bp for side=left, end_bp for side=right), in kb.

refined_vs_c01g_shift_kb.description: Signed shift from the STEP_C01g boundary_{side} call if present; NA otherwise. Lets reviewers compare the two methods directly.

weights_used.description: Per-method weights actually applied in the consensus (recorded for reproducibility).

note_on_side_templating: The library substitutes {side} with 'left' or 'right' at key extraction time based on block_type — matches the pre-existing boundary.schema.json convention.
